title: The Preamble
description: The soul of the Constitution, defining its philosophy and objectives.
points: Source of Authority: 'We, the People of India'.
Nature of State: Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic.

title: Part I: The Union and Its Territory
description: Defines the name and territory of the Union, and the formation of new states.
points: India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States.
Parliament can admit or form new states by law.
Boundaries can be altered without a constitutional amendment.

title: Part III: Fundamental Rights
description: The 'Magna Carta' of India, guaranteed to its citizens.
points: Right to Constitutional Remedies (Art 32) - 'Heart and Soul' per Dr. Ambedkar.

title: Part IV-A: Fundamental Duties
description: The moral obligations of all citizens to help promote a spirit of patriotism and uphold the unity of India.
points: To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals.
To cherish the noble ideals of the national struggle.
To safeguard public property and abjure violence.

title: Schedule 1: States and UTs
description: Lists the names of the States and the Union Territories and their territorial jurisdiction.

title: Schedule 8: Official Languages
description: Lists the 22 official languages recognized by the Constitution.

title: Schedule 10: Anti-Defection
description: Contains provisions as to disqualification on the ground of defection.
